title: All the Championship deals in January transfer window 2026
text: The winter transfer window ran from January 1 through February 2, 2026 at 7pm. Here is a comprehensive summary of Championship club activity: Norwich City made several key moves, bringing in Ali Ahmed from Vancouver Whitecaps for £1.74m and Mohamed Toure from Randers for £2.6m. They also secured Harry Amass on loan from Manchester United and Paris Maghoma from Brentford. Departures included Dylan Jones to Tranmere on loan and Emmanuel Adegboyega to Rotherham. Birmingham City signed Kai Wagner from Philadelphia Union and Carlos Vicente from Alaves for £7m. Notable outgoings included Marc Leonard joining Hearts on loan and Patrick Roberts departing to Sunderland. Southampton experienced significant activity, with Adam Armstrong moving to Wolves for £7m and Armel Bella-Kotchap transferring to Hellas Verona for £4.3m. Ronnie Edwards joined QPR for £4.5m. Derby County acquired Dion Sanderson from Birmingham and Sammie Szmodics on loan from Ipswich, while losing Ebou Adams to Portsmouth for £500k. Sheffield United added Ben Godfrey following his loan spell and secured Kalvin Phillips on loan from Manchester City. Middlesbrough completed deals including Leo Castledine from Chelsea for £1m. Other notable moves involved loan arrangements with Premier League clubs and lower-league signings across all twenty-four Championship clubs.
